title: Ranunculus perfectiflorus
taxonomy: Ranunculus perfectiflorus is a member of the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) and is classified under the following ranks: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Ranunculales, Order: Ranunculales, Family: Ranunculaceae, Genus: Ranunculus, Species: R. perfectiflorus. It is a flowering plant species in the Ranunculus genus, which is primarily composed of annuals or perennials. This classification is based on its characteristics and relationships to other plant species within the kingdom Plantae. The species is a part of the Ranunculales order, which is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in temperate regions. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics among the species within the Ranunculaceae family.

side_effects: Ranunculus perfectiflorus, being a plant native to North America, contains toxic compounds in its tissues, including ranunculin, a glycoside that can cause severe irritation and blistering upon contact with the skin. intro: Ranunculus perfectiflorus, also known as buttercup, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Ranunculaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing, skin conditions, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Ranunculus perfectiflorus include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include tinctures, infusions, and ointments, often used topically to treat various skin conditions and wounds.
